plot_1.summary: Clear scatter plot with appropriate titling and unit-bearing axis labels. The point cloud is easy to read across the small energy-above-hull range and the band-gap range.
issues: Dense clustering near zero on the x-axis causes some over-plotting, slightly obscuring individual points.
Absence of gridlines or light transparency makes it harder to gauge exact positions for heavily clustered data.
Tick marks lack minor divisions, so reading small differences (e.g., below 0.005 eV/atom) is less intuitive.
improvements: Add slight point transparency (alpha) or smaller markers to reduce over-plotting at low x values.
Enable gridlines or minor ticks to improve quantitative readability, especially on the x-axis.
Consider expanding horizontal padding or a log/zoomed inset for the congested region near 0 eV/atom to showcase detail.

plot_2.summary: Easy-to-read scatter; axes have clear numerical scales and proper physical units, and marker size is appropriate. Overall visual clarity is acceptable.
issues: Plot title claims “Density vs Band Gap” but the x-axis label indicates “Energy Above Hull”; this inconsistency is confusing.
No legend or annotation clarifies what the markers represent.
Dense point overlap in the 3–5 eV/atom range may obscure data distribution.
Minor: lack of grid makes it harder to gauge exact values.
improvements: Correct the title (or axis label) so the variables are consistent.
If needed, add a brief legend or caption clarifying the dataset.
Apply slight transparency or jitter to reduce overplotting, or use smaller markers.
Enable a light grid to aid readability.

plot_3.summary: The scatter plot is generally legible, but there is a fundamental labeling mismatch that severely hurts interpretability.
issues: Plot title claims to show Density vs Energy Above Hull, yet the y-axis label is Band Gap (eV); density is nowhere represented.
This contradiction makes it impossible for the viewer to understand what the data truly represent.
Y-axis numerical range (0–0.05 eV) is unusually small for a band-gap plot and may suggest either data truncation or a unit/scale misunderstanding.
No grid lines or minor ticks to aid reading precise locations of dense point clusters.
Points overlap heavily at lower band-gap values, creating visual clutter without transparency or jitter.
improvements: Correct the title or axis labels so they consistently reflect the plotted variables (e.g., rename y-axis to Density or change title to Band Gap vs Energy Above Hull).
Add slight transparency (alpha) or jitter to points or use smaller markers to mitigate overplotting.
Consider enabling grid lines or minor ticks to make value estimation easier.
If the y-axis truly represents band gap, widen the range or explain why the scale is limited to 0–0.05 eV.

plot_4.summary: Histogram conveys the overall distribution and uses a clear title and a properly-labeled x-axis with units.
issues: No y-axis label: reader cannot immediately tell what the bar heights represent.
Number of bins is very high relative to data range, making bars thin and noisy and reducing readability.
Noticeable empty space on the right due to a few far-out values; scaling could be adjusted or those points highlighted separately.
Grid lines or tick marks are sparse, making it a bit harder to gauge exact frequencies.
improvements: Add a y-axis label such as "Count" or "Frequency".
Reduce bin count or use an automatic binning method (e.g., Sturges/Freedman-Diaconis) to produce wider, easier-to-read bars.
Consider x-axis limits or a broken axis / inset to handle distant outliers without large empty space.
Add subtle grid lines or more prominent tick marks to aid value estimation.

plot_5.summary: The histogram is generally clear with a descriptive title and an informative x-axis label that includes units. Bar color and binning look consistent, and the plot is not cluttered.
issues: No y-axis label – readers cannot immediately tell what the counts represent.
Very tall first bin compresses the rest, making smaller populations hard to see; scaling or log/count break might help.
No grid or tick enhancement, reducing ease of value estimation.
Legend is unnecessary but absence of annotation for sample size may leave context vague.
improvements: Add a y-axis label such as "Number of Materials" to clarify the plotted quantity.
Consider log scale on the y-axis, or break the axis, or re-bin to reduce dominance of the zero-energy bar and reveal detail in the tail.
Add light grid lines or stronger tick marks for easier reading.
Optionally note total sample count or percentage to provide context.

validation_notes: Five plots generated: scatter plots for band gap vs. energy above hull, density vs. band gap, and density vs. energy above hull; histograms for band gap and energy above hull distributions.
